pull_request.title: Implement Rust BMAP library and bmapctl CLI
pull_request.body:
## Summary

- Full Rust implementation of the BMAP protocol library (`bmap` crate)
- Same layered architecture as Python: protocol → transport → connection → CLI
- `bmapctl` binary — single 599KB native executable
- `Transport` trait enables mock testing without Bluetooth hardware
- QC Ultra 2 fully supported, QC35 stubbed
- Device configs are data structs with `Option<Addr>` fields (compile-time feature checking)

## Verified against real hardware

```
$ cargo run -- status
  Battery      90%
  Mode         immersion
  CNC          ░░░░░░░░░░ 0/10
  EQ           +0/+0/+0 (bass/mid/treble)
  Name         Fargo
  FW           8.2.20+g34cf029
  Sidetone     medium
  Multipoint   on
  AutoPause    on
  Prompts      off (US English)
```

## Test plan

- [x] `make rust-test` — 41 tests pass (protocol, parsers, devices, connection with mock)
- [x] `make python-test` — 91 tests still pass
- [x] `cargo run -- status/battery/buttons/eq` — verified against QC Ultra 2
- [x] `cargo build --release` — 599KB static binary

changed_files.patch:
@@ -137,21 +137,10 @@ def mode(self):
 
         Returns preset name for known indices, or custom profile name.
         """
-        feat = self._feature("current_mode")
-        fblock, func = feat["addr"]
-        resp = self._transport.send_recv(bmap_packet(fblock, func, OP_GET))
-        parsed = parse_response(resp)
-        if parsed is None:
+        idx = self.mode_idx()
+        if idx is None:
             return None
-        mode_idx = parsed.payload[0]
-        by_idx = self._device.MODE_BY_IDX
-        if mode_idx in by_idx:
-            return by_idx[mode_idx]
-        # Look up custom profile name
-        modes = self.modes()
-        if mode_idx in modes:
-            return modes[mode_idx].name
-        return "unknown(%d)" % mode_idx
+        return self._mode_name_from_idx(idx)
 
     def mode_idx(self):
         """Current audio mode index (int)."""
@@ -163,6 +152,20 @@ def mode_idx(self):
             return parsed.payload[0]
         return None
 
+    def _mode_name_from_idx(self, idx):
+        """Resolve a mode index to a name without an extra GET."""
+        by_idx = self._device.MODE_BY_IDX
+        if idx in by_idx:
+            return by_idx[idx]
+        # Custom profile — need to fetch modes (one drain, not a GET)
+        try:
+            modes = self.modes()
+            if idx in modes:
+                return modes[idx].name
+        except BmapError:
+            pass
+        return "unknown(%d)" % idx
+
     def cnc(self):
         """Noise cancellation (current, max) tuple."""
         return self._get("cnc")
@@ -215,13 +218,16 @@ def modes(self):
 
     def status(self):
         """Full device status snapshot. Returns DeviceStatus namedtuple."""
+        # Single GET for mode index, derive name without extra round trip.
+        current_idx = self.mode_idx()
+        current_name = self._mode_name_from_idx(current_idx) if current_idx is not None else ""
         cnc_cur, cnc_max = self._safe_read(self.cnc, (0, 10))
         prompts_on, prompts_lang = self._safe_read(self.prompts, (False, ""))
 
         return DeviceStatus(
             battery=self.battery(),
-            mode=self.mode(),
-            mode_idx=self.mode_idx(),
+            mode=current_name,
+            mode_idx=current_idx,
             cnc_level=cnc_cur, cnc_max=cnc_max,
             eq=self._safe_read(self.eq, []),
             name=self._safe_read(self.name, ""),

@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# This file is automatically @generated by Cargo.
+# It is not intended for manual editing.
+version = 4
+
+[[package]]
+name = "bmap"
+version = "0.1.0"
+dependencies = [
+ "libc",
+]
+
+[[package]]
+name = "libc"
+version = "0.2.184"
+source = "registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index"
+checksum = "48f5d2a454e16a5ea0f4ced81bd44e4cfc7bd3a507b61887c99fd3538b28e4af"

@@ -4,5 +4,11 @@ version = "0.1.0"
 edition = "2021"
 description = "Control Bluetooth audio devices over the BMAP protocol"
 license = "MIT"
+readme = "../README.md"
 
 [dependencies]
+libc = "0.2"
+
+[[bin]]
+name = "bmapctl"
+path = "src/main.rs"

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+//! Error types for the BMAP library.
+
+use std::fmt;
+
+/// Result type for BMAP operations.
+pub type BmapResult<T> = Result<T, BmapError>;
+
+/// BMAP error types.
+#[derive(Debug)]
+pub enum BmapError {
+    /// Failed to connect to device.
+    Connection(String),
+    /// Device requires authentication.
+    Auth(String),
+    /// Device returned an error response.
+    Device { message: String, code: u8 },
+    /// No response from device.
+    Timeout(String),
+    /// No BMAP device found.
+    NotFound(String),
+    /// Feature not supported by this device.
+    Unsupported(String),
+    /// Invalid argument.
+    InvalidArg(String),
+}
+
+impl fmt::Display for BmapError {
+    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
+        match self {
+            Self::Connection(msg) => write!(f, "Connection error: {}", msg),
+            Self::Auth(msg) => write!(f, "Authentication required: {}", msg),
+            Self::Device { message, code } => write!(f, "Device error {}: {}", code, message),
+            Self::Timeout(msg) => write!(f, "Timeout: {}", msg),
+            Self::NotFound(msg) => write!(f, "Not found: {}", msg),
+            Self::Unsupported(msg) => write!(f, "Unsupported: {}", msg),
+            Self::InvalidArg(msg) => write!(f, "Invalid argument: {}", msg),
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+impl std::error::Error for BmapError {}

commit_summaries.message:
Implement Rust BMAP library and bmapctl CLI

Full Rust implementation of the BMAP protocol with the same layered
architecture as the Python library:

- protocol.rs: Universal BMAP packet encode/decode
- transport.rs: Raw RFCOMM socket via libc (no external BT deps)
- device.rs: Shared parsers and device config structs
- devices.rs: QC Ultra 2 config + QC35 stub
- connection.rs: High-level BmapConnection API
- discovery.rs: Auto-detect paired devices via bluetoothctl
- main.rs: bmapctl CLI — single 599KB binary

24 tests passing. Verified against real QC Ultra 2 headphones:
status, battery, buttons, EQ, mode switching all work.

Fix review findings and add connection tests

- Replace from_raw_fd/forget with direct libc::send/recv (fd safety bug)
- Add Transport trait for dependency injection and testability
- Add MockTransport and 17 connection-layer tests (40 total)
- Fix hex_to_bytes panic on odd-length input
- Document sockaddr_rc layout and repr(C) choice

Fix remaining review informational items

1. Check setsockopt return values (extracted set_sock_timeout helper)
2. Move status_*_offset out of DeviceConfig into device-specific parser
   function (parse_mode_config_qc_ultra2), eliminating QC35 dummy offsets
3. Add set_cnc, set_spatial, set_prompts, set_auto_answer to connection
4. Add profile management (create_profile, delete_profile, ensure_editable)
5. Add modes() and has_feature() to public API
6. Update set_mode to support custom profiles and announce parameter
7. Wire spatial, autoanswer, switch, cnc-set into CLI
8. Add 8 new connection tests (49 total Rust tests)

Fix second review findings

- Fix autoanswer CLI reading auto_pause instead of auto_answer
- Add auto_answer() read method to BmapConnection
- Add EQ range validation (-10 to +10) in set_eq

Eliminate redundant mode GET in status() for both languages

status() was calling mode() + mode_idx() separately, resulting in
2-3 redundant GETs to [31.3] (~400ms wasted at 200ms per round trip).

Now calls mode_idx() once and resolves the name via mode_name_from_idx()
which checks presets first (no I/O) and falls back to modes() only for
custom profiles. Also fixes custom profile name resolution in Rust
(was showing "custom(5)" instead of the actual name).
